body.purpose: Descriptions and content; cap prose at 65-75ch.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Applied to interactive cards (history scans) on hover/focus only; surfaces are flat at rest.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard focus indicator on controls; always visible on :focus-visible.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Ease-out for state transitions; 150-250ms. Conveys state, never decoration.

motion.name: reduced
motion.purpose: Every animation must ship a reduced-motion alternative.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one loud action per view (Analyze, Sign in). Signal Violet fill, near-pill.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-primary"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="16" height="16" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M3 7V5a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h2"/><path d="M17 3h2a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v2"/><path d="M21 17v2a2 2 0 0 1-2 2h-2"/><path d="M7 21H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2v-2"/><circle cx="11.5" cy="11.5" r="2.5"/><path d="M13.27 13.27 16 16"/></svg>Analyze</button>

components.name: Destructive Button
components.description: Subtle by design (Quiet Alert Rule): red text on a 10% red tint, never a solid red fill.

components.name: Verdict Badge — Authentic
components.description: The authentic verdict: Verified Green, always with shield-check icon AND text label (never color alone).
components.html: Authentic

components.name: Verdict Badge — Fake
components.description: The AI/fake verdict: Alert Red tint, with shield-alert icon AND text label. Calm, never a panic banner.
components.html: AI · Fake

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard field: Surface fill, 1px border, violet focus ring (border shift + ring, no glow).

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat container: bright Surface, 1px hairline border, generous rounding. No resting shadow.
components.html:
Detection result
Whether the media is AI-generated or authentic.

components.name: Interactive History Card
components.description: A scan-history thumbnail card: flat at rest, lifts on hover (Flat-By-Default Rule), verdict-tinted border.
components.css: .ds-history{display:block;width:176px;text-align:left;border:1px solid oklch(0.922 0 0);border-radius:12px;background:oklch(1 0 0);overflow:hidden;cursor:pointer;padding:0;transition:transform .18s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1),box-shadow .18s,border-color .18s}.ds-history:hover{transform:translateY(-2px);box-shadow:0 8px 24px oklch(0.145 0 0 / 0.10);border-color:oklch(0.696 0.149 162.5 / 0.5)}.ds-history:focus-visible{outline:none;box-shadow:0 0 0 3px oklch(0.491 0.27 292.581 / 0.3)}.ds-history-thumb{aspect-ratio:16/9;background:oklch(0.97 0 0)}.ds-history-meta{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;gap:8px;padding:10px;font:500 12px/1 'Inter Variable',system-ui,sans-serif;color:oklch(0.145 0 0)}.ds-history-meta span{color:oklch(0.556 0 0);font-weight:400}

narrative.overview: Tabayun is the calm, knowledgeable friend you turn to when you're not sure a photo or video is real. The interface behaves like that friend: it listens (a generous upload), it thinks (an honest loading state), and it tells you straight (one clear verdict, in plain language, no theatrics). Everything serves delivering that verdict faster and making it more believable. The personality is friendly, modern, and reassuring; it refuses both the hypey-AI-product look and the cold enterprise console. Because the subject matter is inherently a little scary, the governing rule is calm over alarm.
keyCharacteristics: Verdict-first: layout, color, and motion serve one legible answer.
Soft & friendly: generous rounding, flat surfaces, gentle hover lifts.
Quietly confident: one accent (Signal Violet), used only where it means something.
Honest, not loud: state colors are muted tints, not full-saturation shouts.
Inclusive by default: verdicts never rely on color alone; AA contrast throughout.

rules.name: The Verdict-Not-By-Color Rule
rules.body: A verdict is never communicated by color alone. Every verdict pairs color with an icon (shield-check / shield-alert) AND an explicit text label. Color-blind users and grayscale renders must read the result perfectly.

rules.name: The Quiet Alert Rule
rules.body: Alert Red appears as a 10-20% tint behind red text and small icons, never as a full-bleed red banner or panic panel. The tool informs; it does not frighten.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Signal Violet covers under 10% of any screen. Its scarcity is what makes the primary action obvious.

rules.name: The One Family Rule
rules.body: Inter only, differentiated by weight and size. No display or decorative fonts in UI labels, buttons, or data. Fixed rem scale, not fluid clamp.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat with a 1px border at rest. Shadow and lift are earned by interaction (hover, focus), never applied decoratively to static panels.

dos: Do treat the verdict as the product — make the answer the most legible thing on the screen.
Do pair every verdict with an icon and a text label, so it reads in grayscale and for color-blind users.
Do keep Signal Violet to under 10% of a screen, reserved for the primary action, active state, and focus.
Do deliver 'fake' results calmly — Alert Red as a tint behind red text, with a steady explanatory sentence.
Do use Inter at one weight/size scale; let hierarchy come from weight and size, not new fonts.
Do keep surfaces flat with a 1px border at rest; reserve shadow/lift for hover and focus.
Do ensure body and muted text clear 4.5:1 against Surface; never drop body copy to light gray.
Do promote the hardcoded emerald authentic color to a real semantic token for consistency.
donts: Don't use gradient text, glassmorphism, glowing orbs, or neon AI theater — this is not a hypey AI startup.
Don't build cold, gray-on-gray enterprise density or intimidating data walls.
Don't slip into the alarmist security/hacker register: no full-bleed red banners, no fear-driven dark cyber theming.
Don't ship the generic shadcn-default look — Signal Violet is an owned identity, not an untouched template accent.
Don't convey a verdict (or any critical state) by color alone.
Don't use border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't introduce a display/decorative font into UI labels, buttons, or data.
Don't animate decoratively; motion conveys state (150-250ms) and always has a prefers-reduced-motion alternative.
